هو المقصود به اليقين يعني يوقنون
بلقاء الله لكن استعمال الظن هنا له اسباب
another access point of you cannysically
نسال الله سبحانه وتعالى ان يعلمنا ما
ينفعنا وان ينفعنا بما علمنا نساله تعالى
ان يزيدنا علما ونساله سبحانه ان يجعلنا
هداه مهديين والله اعلم وصلى الله وسلم

summary: This lecture from the Muslim Community Center of Tucson provides an Islamic educational discussion on the definitions and distinctions between key Islamic concepts, particularly focusing on Islam (submission to Allah), Iman (faith/belief), and Tassadeeq (confirmation). The instructor uses Riyadul Saliheen (Gardens of the Righteous) as a reference and explores the linguistic and Shariah definitions of these terms. The lecture emphasizes that Iman encompasses not just belief in the heart, but also verbal affirmation and righteous actions, distinguishing it from mere intellectual assent. The discussion includes references to Quranic verses and examples from Islamic history to illustrate how true belief must be accompanied by conviction without doubt and must lead to corresponding actions.
suggested_title: Islamic Definitions: Understanding Islam, Iman, and Tassadeeq - Lecture on Fundamentals of Faith
hadith_chapter: Chapter on Taqwa (God-consciousness) and Monitoring of Allah
catchy_title: Islam, Iman, Tassadeeq: What's the Difference?
